required_skills: Real-time simulation development using game engines (Unreal Engine, Unity) or dedicated simulation frameworks (VBS4, Presagis, FlightGear)
Physics simulation programming including rigid body dynamics, fluid dynamics approximations, vehicle dynamics models, and projectile ballistics
C++ or C# programming for high-performance simulation logic, deterministic state management, and real-time system integration
Mathematical modeling and numerical methods including Euler integration, Runge-Kutta solvers, and physics-based equation of motion implementation
Distributed simulation protocols including DIS (Distributed Interactive Simulation) and HLA (High Level Architecture) for multi-entity networked simulation
Sensor and environmental modeling including radar cross-section, LIDAR simulation, weather effects, and terrain interaction systems
Real-time performance engineering ensuring simulation fidelity targets are met within strict latency and frame-rate budgets on target hardware
Systems integration with hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) setups, motion platforms, cockpit interfaces, and external data feeds
preferred_skills: Aerodynamics and flight dynamics modeling (6DOF flight models) for fixed-wing, rotary-wing, and UAV simulation applications
Autonomous vehicle and robotics simulation development including sensor fusion simulation, path planning validation, and ADAS testing environments
Machine learning integration for AI agent behavior in simulation environments using reinforcement learning (RL) frameworks
Constructive and virtual simulation integration including OPFOR AI scripting, mission scripting, and AAR (After Action Review) system development
Synthetic environment development including correlated terrain databases, georeferenced imagery, and OTW (Out-The-Window) visual database generation
Real-time streaming and telemetry data integration from external systems including GPS, IMU, and EO/IR sensor feeds
DO-178C or MIL-STD-1472 compliance awareness for safety-critical simulation system development in defense or aerospace domains
responsibilities: Design and implement real-time physics simulation systems for vehicles, aircraft, projectiles, and environmental interactions with high-fidelity accuracy requirements
Develop 6DOF flight dynamics models and vehicle dynamics systems for training simulators, integrating aerodynamic coefficient data and validated against real-world performance envelopes
Implement distributed simulation architectures using HLA federation management and DIS protocol entity state broadcasting for multi-node, networked simulation environments
Build and integrate sensor simulation modules including radar, LIDAR, EO/IR, and GPS degradation models that accurately replicate sensor behavior under varied environmental conditions
Optimize real-time simulation performance to achieve deterministic tick rates and sub-frame latency targets required for hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) and motion platform integration
Develop correlated synthetic environment databases and georeferenced terrain systems that support accurate geospatial positioning and culturally authentic visual scene generation
Integrate machine learning-driven AI agents and autonomous system behaviors into simulation environments for scenario automation, OPFOR behavior, and RL-based agent training
Collaborate with domain subject matter experts (pilots, vehicle operators, systems engineers) to validate simulation fidelity against operational data and real-world performance benchmarks
Design and implement After Action Review (AAR) systems that record, replay, and annotate simulation session data for debriefing, performance assessment, and training analytics
Support system integration testing of simulation platforms with external hardware including motion bases, collimated display systems, cockpit replicas, and live telemetry data feeds
